test_description: After masked monopole/dipole subtraction, fit a band-limited temperature field on the unmasked sky to obtain a_lm for 2<=l<=20, using the identical mask and fitting pipeline for SMICA and all simulations. For each trial plane normal n on a fixed antipodally identified HEALPix grid, e.g. nside=8, rotate the a_lm so n is the z-axis, then compute P_even(n)=sum |a_lm^(n)|^2 over modes with l+m even and P_odd(n)=sum |a_lm^(n)|^2 over modes with l+m odd. The single scalar is S=max_n |ln[(P_even(n)+epsilon)/(P_odd(n)+epsilon)]| with a fixed tiny epsilon; compare the SMICA value to the 1000 LCDM values using the same axis grid and maximization.
test_hypothesis: SMICA has an unusually large optimized low-\(\ell\) mirror-parity power imbalance between mirror-even and mirror-odd modes compared with LCDM simulations.
justificaton: The statistic \(S=\max_n |\log((P_{\rm even}+\epsilon)/(P_{\rm odd}+\epsilon))|\) measures the magnitude of the imbalance, with the sign already removed. The code explicitly treats larger \(S\) as more anomalous and evaluates the upper tail, so only unusually high values support the anomaly hypothesis.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Mirror-Parity Plane Log-Imbalance: after masked monopole/dipole subtraction, estimate the 2<=ell<=20 temperature multipoles with the same finite-filled masked-sky pipeline for SMICA and simulations; on a fixed antipodally identified HEALPix nside=8 grid of plane normals, split the low-l field into mirror-even and mirror-odd parts for each plane and return S=max_n |ln((P_even+epsilon)/(P_odd+epsilon))|.
custom_summary.statistic: S = max over antipodally identified plane normals of |log((P_even+epsilon)/(P_odd+epsilon))|
custom_summary.ell_range: 2 <= ell <= 20, reduced only if the input nside is too small
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail: larger S means a stronger optimized mirror-even/mirror-odd low-l power imbalance

plot_spec.title: Low-l mirror-parity plane log-imbalance
plot_spec.caption: Simulation distribution of the optimized low-ell mirror-parity log-imbalance; the observed SMICA value is assessed in the upper tail.

custom_summary.interpretation: SMICA is not in the highest 5% tail for this optimized low-l mirror-parity imbalance.

test_summary.Description: SMICA and 1000 matched LCDM simulations were masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted, fitted for 2<=ell<=20 coefficients, rotated over an antipodally identified nside=8 plane grid, and scored with S=max_n |ln[(P_even+epsilon)/(P_odd+epsilon)]| for l+m mirror parity.
test_summary.Results: SMICA S=0.8446 versus simulation mean 0.9600, median 0.9394, std 0.1711. Its midrank percentile is 26.9%; 731/1000 simulations have larger S, giving an upper-tail p=0.731, about -0.67 sigma relative to the simulation mean.
test_summary.Interpretation: The observed optimized mirror-even/mirror-odd low-l power imbalance is not unusually large; it is below the typical simulation value and far from the upper 5% anomaly region.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Broad low-l parity anomalies are discussed in Planck Collaboration XVI (2016, A&A 594, A16) and VII (2020, A&A 641, A7), but the supplied/previous searches found no directly comparable published test using this exact optimized log-imbalance, mask-fitting pipeline, ell range and grid.
